The Division of Korea

# The Division of Korea

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Jun 25, 2021

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

[Korea](https://www.quora.com/Why-was-Korea-split-into-North-and-South-after-WW2/answer/Ratnakar-Sadasyula-%E0%B0%B0%E0%B0%A4%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%A8%E0%B0%BE%E0%B0%95%E0%B0%B0%E0%B1%8D-%E0%B0%B8%E0%B0%A6%E0%B0%B8%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%AF%E0%B1%81%E0%B0%B2) was a Japanese colony since 1910, after the Russo-Japanese war ended. The ruler Gojong was deposed, and there was a long resistance movement against Japanese occupation. However the rather fragmented nature of the movement, ensured the Japanese managed to assert their control over Korea. Even the Korean Provisional Government in exile in China, could not get the desired recognition.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!NPaS!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Feec569f1-d825-4654-9f46-ac5aded75047_580x442.webp)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!NPaS!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Feec569f1-d825-4654-9f46-ac5aded75047_580x442.webp)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The Cairo conference of 1943, during the War, attended by Winston Churchill, F.D.Roosevelt, and Chiang Kai Shek, discussed at length on what to do with the territories under Japanese occupation. Mention was also made of Korea’s occupation by Japan, and that it would be independent in due course of time. With the Soviet Union committing to join the Pacific War, post the Teheran(1943) and Yalta(1945) conferences the stage was set. After its victory in Europe, the Soviet Union declared War on Japan on August 8, 1945, and soon they rapidly advanced into Asia.

Concerned about growing Soviet influence in Asia, the US Government asked two young officers, Dean Rusk and Charles Bonesteel, to define the American occupation zone. The problem was both of them had no knowledge of Korea, and their only point of reference was a National Geographic map. With time running short, the two men, hastily decided the 38th parallel as the boundary, thus effectively splitting Korea into two halves. It was a completely hasty decision imposed on Korea, by two men, who had no idea of the place. Nor did they consult any experts on Korea. Their only point was that south of 38th Parallel, Seoul would come under American control. The fact is that much earlier at the turn of the 20th century, both Japan and Russia had a similar plan, during the war between them. Rusk later told that had he known about this, he would have had chosen a totally different line. While 16 million Koreans were in the American part, around 9 million were there in the Soviet section. And much to the surprise of US, the Soviet Union actually accepted the division. This agreement to divide Korea was incorporated into GO No 1, signed on the surrender of Japan.

With the War coming to an end, the Independence movement in Korea, now gathered steam. Abe Nobuyuki ([Nobuyuki Abe - Wikipedia](https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nobuyuki_Abe "en.m.wikipedia.org")) the last Japanese Governor General of Korea, laid the ground for the hand over of power, he had good contacts with the elite there. The short lived People’s Republic of Korea was formed in September 1945. In the meanwhile at the 1945 Moscow Conference, the Allies agreed that USSR, US, Britain , France and China would look over Korea, through a trusteeship of sorts, for 5 years. However most Koreans wanted complete independence, and rejected the idea, except the Communist parties there. Also increasing tensions between the US and USSR, coupled with the opposition to trusteeship, ensured that the proposal failed. By May 1946, it became illegal to cross the 38th parallel without a permit.

Fearing a Soviet take over, Douglas McArthur announced that Lt.Gen John Hodge would oversee the Korean affairs, and deputed him there in September 1945. Hodge refused to meet representatives of the Provisional Government in China, nor those of the People’s Republic. There were protests in September 1946, by workers and peasants, against the US imposed military Government, which were however put down. The communists were crushed, their most prominent leader Lyuh Woon-Hyung was assassinated. Synghman Rhee took control of Southern Korea, and more than 100,000 people were killed in a crackdown on communists. On the other hand, the Soviets entered Pyongyang, and gave full freedom to the various People’s Committees to function. The provisional government implemented a sweeping land reform program, where land that once belonged to the Japanese was taken and redistributed. Many farmers and landlords fled to the South as the program was implemented.

With not much progress, the US brought the Korean issue to the UN in 1947. Owing to US influence, the UN passed a resolution demanding elections in Korea, withdrawal of foreign troops and a commission would be set up. With the Soviets boycotting the elections, it was decided to hold elections in the South only. However many Koreans did not accept the elections, feeling they were a ploy to divide the country. By February 1948, there were strikes all over South Korea against the decision. A large uprising in Jeju island was put down brutally by the South Korean troops.

Many prominent politicians down South boycotted the elections. Kim Koo, an activist for unification and last leader of the Provisional Govt, was assassinated. The UN managed to hold the elections in May 1948, the US backed Syngman Rhee won and became the first President of South Korea. The People’s Democratic Republic of Korea in the North was founded on September 9, 1948 with Kim Il-Sung as it’s Prime Minister. The division was now complete.
